stories.title: Knicks 115-104 Cavaliers — ECF Game 1 (OT, May 19) — Brunson's 22-Point Comeback
stories.angle: Brunson attacked Harden relentlessly in the fourth quarter; Mitchell went silent after the 8:19 mark; Knicks outscored Cavs 14-3 in overtime.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
DOWN 22 WITH 7:52 LEFT.

Brunson erupted for 38 points — 15 in the fourth quarter — as the Knicks went on a 44-11 run and stole Game 1.

Knicks 115, Cavaliers 104 (OT). ECF: NYK leads 1-0.

#HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Mitchell went silent at the 8-min mark. Harden: 1-of-8 from three, 6 turnovers.

Knicks coach targeted Harden every possession down the stretch.

The formula worked. 115-104 OT. Knicks up 1-0 in the ECF.

#HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
They were down 22. The clock showed 7:52. Madison Square Garden had gone quiet. Cleveland looked like they had the Eastern Conference Finals locked up.

Then Jalen Brunson decided it wasn't over.

Brunson went to work in a way that will be talked about for years. He scored 15 points in the final minutes of the fourth quarter alone — attacking switches, drawing fouls, and refusing to let the Knicks die. When the game needed a hero with 20 seconds left, he hit a floater off the glass to tie it. The Knicks then outscored Cleveland 14-3 in overtime.

Final: Knicks 115, Cavaliers 104 (OT). ECF series: New York leads 1-0.

The story of the collapse is just as important as the comeback. Donovan Mitchell put up 29 points but went silent in the final eight minutes when the Cavaliers needed him most. James Harden was targeted relentlessly by Knicks coach Mike Brown, and he delivered exactly what New York was hoping for — 1-of-8 from three-point range and six turnovers as Cleveland's offense fell apart.

The Knicks lead. Game 2 is TONIGHT at MSG, 8 PM ET on ESPN.
facebook.image_caption:
Jalen Brunson. 38 points. A 22-point comeback. Overtime. A stolen Game 1.

MSG was electric and the Cavaliers couldn't handle it.

Knicks lead the ECF 1-0. Game 2 TONIGHT. #HoopHeroes #Knicks

article.headline: Brunson's 38-Point Masterpiece Completes Knicks' 22-Point ECF Game 1 Comeback Over Cavaliers

stories.title: Thunder 122-113 Spurs — WCF Game 2 (May 20) — SGA Responds, Series Tied 1-1
stories.angle: SGA dominant but both teams lost key players to injury (Williams and Harper) mid-game.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
SGA responds. OKC levels the series.

30 pts. 9 ast. +14. Thunder 122, Spurs 113.

Wemby had 21/17. Castle put up 28/8. But 21 Spurs turnovers — and two key injuries — sealed it.

WCF tied 1-1.

#HoopHeroes #Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Spurs had 21 turnovers in Game 2. OKC had 9.

SGA exploited every one of them. 30 points, 9 assists, +14.

Thunder 122-113. Series tied. Game 3 Friday in San Antonio, 8:30 PM ET.

#HoopHeroes #Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander answered back. Oklahoma City answered back. And the Western Conference Finals is now tied.

The Thunder defeated the San Antonio Spurs 122-113 in WCF Game 2 at Paycom Center on Wednesday night, with SGA delivering 30 points, 9 assists, and a +14 finish on 50 percent shooting. It was the kind of performance that reminded everyone why he's a two-time MVP — focused, decisive, and in command.

But the margin between these two teams wasn't just about stars. The Spurs committed 21 turnovers — the Thunder only 9. Those 12 extra possessions told the story of San Antonio's second-half frustrations, and OKC made every one of them count.

Still, the Spurs showed fight. Victor Wembanyama went for 21 points and 17 rebounds, and Stephon Castle — stepping in for the injured De'Aaron Fox — posted 28 points and 8 assists. The foundation is there.

The injury news looms large. Both Jalen Williams (hamstring) and Dylan Harper (right leg) exited Game 2 early. Combined with Fox's ankle injury, the Spurs are heading to San Antonio for Game 3 with serious roster questions.

Series tied 1-1. Game 3: Friday May 22, 8:30 PM ET, AT&T Center, San Antonio, NBC/Peacock.
facebook.image_caption:
SGA back to doing what he does. 30 points. 9 assists. OKC responds.

Thunder 122, Spurs 113. WCF tied 1-1 — but the injury news is what everyone is talking about. #HoopHeroes #Thunder

article.headline: SGA's 30-Point Response Lifts Thunder Past Spurs to Even WCF Series at 1-1

stories.title: WCF Injury Watch — Harper, Williams, Fox All Uncertain for Game 3
stories.angle: Spurs heading to San Antonio potentially without three key contributors; Wembanyama may need to carry an even heavier load in Game 3.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Jalen Williams (hamstring). Dylan Harper (right leg). De'Aaron Fox (ankle).

All three are uncertain for WCF Game 3 Friday in San Antonio.

The Spurs need answers — fast.

#HoopHeroes #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Three key players. Three concerning injuries. One enormous series suddenly hanging in the balance.

The San Antonio Spurs entered WCF Game 2 already without their starting point guard — De'Aaron Fox has been sidelined since suffering a right high ankle sprain in the second round against Minnesota, and coach Mitch Johnson said status decisions will be made day by day.

Then it got worse. Jalen Williams, the Thunder's second-best player, left Game 2 in the first half with tightness in the same left hamstring that had cost him six games earlier in the playoffs. He did not return.

Stephon Castle — already filling in for Fox — lost his backcourt partner Dylan Harper in the third quarter. Harper landed awkwardly on his right leg twice while challenging SGA at the rim and going for a rebound. He was ruled out for the fourth quarter and did not return.

Neither team gave post-game injury updates. Nobody knows who plays Friday.

What we do know: Wembanyama has averaged 31 points and 20.5 rebounds through two WCF games. He will be asked to carry even more. The question is whether the humans around him are healthy enough to help.

WCF Game 3: Friday May 22, 8:30 PM ET, AT&T Center, San Antonio, NBC/Peacock.
facebook.image_caption:
Three critical injuries. One massive series. The WCF just got a lot more unpredictable.

Fox (ankle). Harper (leg). Williams (hamstring). All uncertain for Game 3.

Wembanyama will carry San Antonio — but how much can one player do? #HoopHeroes #Spurs

stories.title: ECF Game 2 Preview — Cavaliers Must Fix Harden Problem TONIGHT
stories.angle: Cleveland blew a 22-point lead; Brunson will attack Harden switches again; Cavs are 0-1 and can't afford a 2-0 hole going into Games 3-4 at MSG.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
TONIGHT. MSG. 8 PM ET. ESPN.

The Cavaliers need to answer Brunson's 38-point masterpiece — and fix the Harden problem.

ECF Game 2: Can Cleveland respond?

#HoopHeroes #Cavaliers #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
TONIGHT at Madison Square Garden.

The Cleveland Cavaliers go back to New York needing to recalibrate after one of the more stunning collapses in recent playoff memory. They led by 22 in the fourth quarter. They had the crowd beaten. They had Donovan Mitchell rolling. And then it was gone.

The diagnosis isn't complicated: James Harden needs to be better. He was targeted on defense throughout the fourth quarter, and he delivered 1-of-8 from three and six turnovers. The Cavs went from cruising to panicking, and Harden couldn't stop the bleeding. Cleveland has publicly backed their veteran guard, but something has to change.

Mitchell needs to stay engaged past the three-quarter mark. His 29 points were efficient and impressive — but they came in the first three quarters. When the Knicks made their push, he had already deposited his production for the night.

Meanwhile, Jalen Brunson's formula worked perfectly. He will attack Harden again tonight. Can Cleveland fix it?

ECF Game 2: May 21, 8:00 PM ET, Madison Square Garden, ESPN. Series: Knicks lead 1-0.
facebook.image_caption:
TONIGHT. MSG. 8 PM ET on ESPN.

The Cavaliers need to respond. Brunson is waiting.

ECF Game 2 — Can Cleveland recalibrate? #HoopHeroes #Cavaliers

article.headline: ECF Game 2 Preview: Cavaliers Must Fix the Harden Problem or Fall Into a 0-2 Hole at MSG

stories.title: WCF Game 3 Preview — Series Shifts to San Antonio, Wemby Must Carry Load
stories.angle: Home crowd will be a factor; Wemby averaging 31 pts/20.5 reb in series; SGA on 30-point game and riding momentum.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
WCF shifts to San Antonio. Series tied 1-1.

Spurs could be without Fox, Harper, AND Williams for Game 3 Friday.

Wembanyama will need to carry the load — again.

8:30 PM ET, May 22, NBC/Peacock.

#HoopHeroes #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Western Conference Finals were billed as a heavyweight showdown between two of basketball's most gifted young stars. Through two games, Victor Wembanyama and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander have delivered on that promise — but the injury cloud over San Antonio is threatening to change the series entirely.

The Spurs head home to AT&T Center for Game 3 on Friday night with three key contributors in various degrees of doubt. De'Aaron Fox has been out with his ankle since the second round. Dylan Harper left Game 2 with a right leg injury. Jalen Williams — who missed six playoff games earlier this season — exited with hamstring tightness.

Wembanyama averaged 31 points and 20.5 rebounds through the first two games. In a healthy series, that might be enough to keep things interesting. Against a Thunder team that had only 9 turnovers in Game 2 while San Antonio had 21, the margin for error is thin.

Oklahoma City comes in with confidence and a tied series. SGA just had his best game of the WCF. The Thunder defense is sharp.

But San Antonio has home court for Game 3. The crowd will be loud. Wembanyama will be motivated. And playoff basketball has a way of defying rosters.

WCF Game 3: Friday May 22, 8:30 PM ET, AT&T Center, San Antonio, NBC/Peacock.
facebook.image_caption:
Game 3. San Antonio. Friday night.

The WCF is tied 1-1, but the Spurs' injury situation puts their season in serious danger.

Wembanyama vs. SGA. Can Wemby carry San Antonio home? #HoopHeroes #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: WCF Game 3 Preview: Spurs Head Home With Injury Questions and Wembanyama's Back Against the Wall
